items.title: The dry heart of Australia is now awash with water

children.content: The myth of an inland sea lured 19th century European explorers into the heart of Australia. But where they found only desert, this year it's awash, with water levels at Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre now the best some locals have ever seen.

articles.title: Heavy rain to soak Australia's east as un-El-Niño-like weather continues

children.content: For an El Niño year, 2026 is so far the wettest on record for Australia, and an influx of tropical moisture is about to deliver another round of widespread rain.

image.altText: a rainbow and heavy dark clouds over the water at port macquarie

Hundreds of pelicans sitting down on flat, brown ground
An aerial photo of Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre

items.title: Volunteers say hotline told them to collect dead bird against government advice

children.content: South Australian citizen scientists say they called the Emergency Animal Disease Hotline to report a dead sea bird and were given advice that contradicts official government messaging.

image.altText: A dead pied cormorant in shallow beach water

items.title: Olive industry 'buzzing' as diets fuel demand, farmers diversify

children.content: Consumer interest in olive oil has boomed at the same time as growing the produce has flourished, leading to excitement in the industry.

image.altText: A man holds an olive branch up to the camera.

items.title: No-show cuttlefish result in million-dollar loss for regional SA town

children.content: Whyalla has been forced to wind back services and let staff let go as its tourism industry suffers million-dollar losses from cuttlefish not making their usual migration.

image.altText: Two colourful sea creatures swimming among the vegetation in the ocean

items.title: South Australia's First Nations Voice to Parliament reforms revealed

children.content: South Australia's First Nations Voice to Parliament reforms are being revealed.

image.altText: A woman wearing a scarf holding a young female child

items.title: SA Police officers 'did not act appropriately' in handling of DV survivor's case

children.content: South Australia Police has admitted that three officers did not "act appropriately" in how they handled the case of domestic violence victim Stella Magnisalis, who was assaulted multiple times by her then police detective husband.

articles.title: Woman fights to have ex-policeman husband kept behind bars

children.content: A domestic violence survivor speaks out about her fight to keep her former detective ex-husband behind bars as he attempts to be released on parole.

image.altText: A woman with black haair close-up looking sad

gallery.altText: A woman looks to the right sadly in front of an SA Police logo

items.title: 'Glint in their eyes': How Aussies watched the Socceroos match against Paraguay

children.content: Crowds across the nation adorned in green and gold gathered in support of the Socceroos for their third match in the FIFA World Cup.

image.altText: A man wearing black sunglasses and a full-body kangaroo costume with a Socceroos scarf around his neck shows of his muscles.

items.title: Death of man who held pregnant wife hostage 'not preventable', coroner finds

children.content: Inquest findings have been handed down into the death of Matthew Kim Morgan, who was shot by police during a siege, in which he was holding his pregnant wife hostage, at the Tollgate Motel at Glen Osmond in 2017.

image.altText: A long gun on a tiled floor

items.title: Vets turn away wild birds as avian influenza detected in SA

children.content: Wildlife rescuers say they need help to prepare for a potential bird flu outbreak as vets issue statements saying they can no longer take in sick or injured wild birds.

image.altText: Mish Simpson woman with green jacket and blonde hair smiling

items.title: Another suspected H5 bird flu case detected in WA

children.content: Another suspected positive case of the H5 bird flu has been detected in Western Australia, marking the fifth confirmed or suspected case in Australia.

image.altText: A woman with shoulder-length hair is speaking to media.

items.title: Triple fatality prompts medical certificate call for learner drivers

children.content: South Australia's deputy coroner recommends learner drivers obtain a medical certificate affirming their ability to drive before they get a licence.

gallery.altText: A woman leans against an older man in a hat and striped shirt and an older woman wearing glasses, all smiling.

items.title: Antic's 'dangerous' comments on vaccinations condemned

children.content: South Australian Liberal senator Alex Antic has been roundly condemned over comments he made about vaccinations after avian influenza was found on Australia's southern coast.

image.altText: A close up of Alex Antic speaking in the Senate chamber, in a suit with a red tie

items.title: Animal cruelty charges laid a year on from piggery footage

children.content: A year after footage from the site was widely circulated the RSPCA has pressed animal cruelty charges against people connected to Andgar Piggery.
Warning: This article contains graphic images.

image.altText: A pig with a dark background behind it.

items.title: NBL fines Bogut over alleged body-shaming comments to 36ers owner

children.content: The NBL hands Andrew Bogut a fine of $9,500 over comments he allegedly made towards Adelaide 36ers owner Grant Kelley.

image.altText: A man looks a little bit annoyed while watching a basketball game from the sidelines.

items.title: Kangaroo Island declared feral pig-free after more than two centuries

children.content: Devastating bushfires provide Kangaroo Island with a unique silver lining as it becomes the largest island in the world to eradicate feral pigs.

image.altText: A dark coloured feral pig in bushland

items.title: Australia's next opponent locked in after World Cup chaos

children.content: The Socceroos will meet Egypt in the knockout stage of the World Cup after a stunning finish in Group G. Follow live.

articles.title: Live table: Which third-placed teams will make it to the knockouts?

articles.title: Departure of Leckie and Italiano leaves Socceroos 'two soldiers down'

children.content: Australia enters the World Cup knockout rounds with just 24 players in the squad after veteran Mathew Leckie and right-back Jacob Italiano departed the camp with injuries.

items.title: 'You can hear them': Thousands of dung beetles exchanged via post

children.content: While some dung beetles rest, others get to work, and farmers are making trades.

items.title: Magpies face struggling Tigers as Blues celebrate sixth straight win

children.content: Carlton makes it six straight wins under interim coach Josh Fraser with a big victory over West Coast, ahead of the 60th edition of the Showdown in Adelaide tonight. Follow live.

image.altText: Nick Daicos pumps his fist

items.title: In Beirut, a sacred ceremony also becomes display of Hezbollah's force

children.content: Tens of thousands of Shiite Muslims gathered in Beirut for the largest Ashura procession in years, as Hezbollah used the holy day to project strength despite growing pressure to disarm after months of war with Israel.

gallery.altText: Dozens of men, sitting cross-legged, all of them wearing black. Some are wearing white turbans.

thumbnail.altText: Dozens of men, sitting cross-legged, all of them wearing black. Some are wearing white turbans.

items.title: Crime scene declared after armed man shot by police south of Brisbane

children.content: An armed man has been shot by police south of Brisbane after he allegedly confronted officers while carrying a rifle.

image.altText: Police tape in focus in front of a cream and blue house.

items.title: 'Systemic issue': Abuse sees Canberra referees quitting after a year

children.content: Canberra soccer match officials say referee abuse is a "systemic issue" across all sporting codes, with increasingly hostile behaviour from spectators, players and coaches causing 44 per cent of Capital Football's referees to leave their roles after a year.

image.altText: A man in a cap stands on a soccer field talking to three youth referees.

items.title: Heartbreaking scenes emerging from earthquakes in Venezuela

children.content: After two powerful earthquakes, rescue teams from around the globe are in a race against time to pull survivors from the rubble.

articles.title: Why Venezuela doesn't have an 'earthquake preparedness culture'

children.content: As a rare one-two punch of earthquakes hit Venezuela this week, residents in the capital Caracas watched in horror as buildings shook and were sent "pancaking" before their eyes.

image.altText: A man involved in rescue efforts looks on from a damaged building in Venezuela.

items.title: At 80, John Williamson is ready to hang up his guitar

children.content: Now that folk and country music legend John Williamson is 80, he wants to spend less time touring and more time at home eating his own tomatoes.

items.title: A warm and comforting gnocchi bake

children.content: "This would be something I'd love to make on a Sunday and have it bubbling away while you're doing odd jobs around the house."

items.title: Shock results from peptide test reveal 'life-threatening' ingredients

children.content: Doctors warn of an "enormous" risk of overdose after testing of a popular unapproved weight loss peptide reveals the product had double the strength of what was listed on its label.

items.title: Described as a 'talented athlete', Hussein Dehaini was gunned down at 33

children.content: Those who knew Hussein Dehaini say he could have been anything he wanted to be. But his life ended at 33, when he was gunned down in the middle of a Melbourne street.

items.title: One of the deadliest cancers in Australia is becoming more common

children.content: Clinical worker John McNeur didn't realise he was unwell until he was delivering a health workshop on pancreatic cancer in the remote Northern Territory — and he began ticking all of the boxes. Now he's on a mission to raise awareness.

items.title: Thousands in south-east Qld without power after attempted copper theft

children.content: Energex says it appears someone broke into a substation's cable pit and attempted to cut through an 11,000-volt cable, leaving about 13,000 south-east Queensland customers without power overnight.

children.content: Cabo Verde has secured the most unlikely spot in the World Cup knockouts, and the nation of 500,000 people is daring to dream.

items.title: Sect leader convicted after girls found in trailer on US highway

children.content: A polygamous sect leader already serving a 50-year prison sentence in the US has been convicted of child abuse after girls were found in an unventilated trailer he was hauling through Arizona.
